# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.
# Category: datastore_security

version: '2' # the docker-compose version, here we use it to define services in versions (here single service) which makes sure that all parts of configuration are compatible with each other – so they can be updated/removed if needed. The following two lines is mandatory and will ensure compatibility for this file
services:     # the definition begins after these comments, here we have a redis server setup to handle our application data as well as debugging connections between servers   (here single service) – so it can run in parallel with other services or serve its own purpose. This is how you define new instances of such an instance
    app:         # the name defined for this service, here we have given a descriptive and unique identifier to our application server which makes maintaining/debugging easier (here single-service) – can be named anything whatever your project requirements allow as per naming convention. Here redis is used instead of any other type
      image: 'redis' # the Dockerimage in use, here we have defined a dockerized Redis server which matches our application requirement or setup (here single service) – can be named anything whatever your project requirements allow as per naming convention. Here redis is used instead of any other type
      ports:        # defining all exposed services to the outside world and where they should come from, here we have defined a port for our application server which makes sure that connections are accessible only within this network (here single service) – can be named anything whatever your project requirements allow as per naming convention. Here redis is used instead of any other type
        - '6379:localhost' # the services in-use and where they should come from, here we have defined a port for our application server which makes sure that connections are accessible only within this network (here single service) – can be named anything whatever your project requirements allow as per naming convention. Here redis is used instead of any other type
